pr.title: Match item callbacks and misc functions
pr.author: malvarezcastillo

summary: Matched roughly 70 previously stubbed item callbacks and miscellaneous fighter/stage functions, mostly across common item files plus Samus grapple, Kirby copy-hat initialization, and ground animation helpers. The PR also refined several item and fighter structs so callbacks could use concrete fields instead of placeholders, notably adding Samus grapple-specific motion vars to avoid incorrectly treating a shared union field as both integer and float. Review focused on distinguishing real type/inline discoveries from fake-match documentation and on using per-state union members rather than type-punning shared motion vars.
what_changed: Implemented several Samus grapple item functions in itsamusgrapple.c, including cleanup of linked hookshot objects, velocity/state transition helpers, callback cleanup/dispatch, and setting grapple duration from Samus data attributes.
Added a grapple-specific member to union ftSamus_MotionVars with x4 typed as float, while preserving the existing SpecialN state member where x4 remains an integer frame counter.

Matched five Kirby-related misc functions in ftKb_Init.c, including Kirby hat loading for normal Kirby and Dr. Mario copy ability, callback motion-state changes, and a simple specialn_kp_flame_scale accessor.
Implemented grAnime_801C787C and exposed it in granime.h; the function builds a flag mask from arg2 bits and calls grAnime_801C752C on a ground JObj.

Added and refined item structs in itCommonItems.h and itCharItems.h, including itWstar_ItemVars, itFlipper_DatAttrs, itHarisen_DatAttrs, added fields for box/flipper/Samus grapple vars, and corrected Samus grapple xC from f32 to HSD_JObj*.
Matched item callbacks for box, flipper, harisen, lipstick, octarock stone, taru, tarucann, and warp star according to the PR context, replacing multiple placeholder comments with concrete functions.
Updated prototypes for more specific typing, such as it_8029A218 in itlipstickspore.h, it_802E89D0 in itoctarockstone.h, grAnime_801C787C in granime.h, and it_802B75FC in itsamusgrapple.h.
smart_moves: Resolved the Samus grapple type-pun problem by adding a per-state union member instead of globally changing unk3.x4 from s32 to float, which would have broken SpecialN codegen.

Verified the consequence of changing Samus unk3.x4 to float: it caused stfs instead of stw in ftSs_SpecialN_Enter, ftSs_SpecialAirN_Enter, and ftSs_SpecialNHold_Anim, proving the field is state-dependent.

Recognized that Samus grapple and SpecialN share ftSamus_MotionVars storage but require different semantic layouts, following the reviewer-suggested Yoshi-style pattern of one union struct per action state.
Converted an explanatory @note about a harisen double user_data load into an explicit @todo fake-match note after review, making the questionable matching tactic searchable and honest.

Documented the suspected inline boundary in it_3F14_Logic24_Dropped with concrete evidence: separate gobj->user_data loads and the likely inline pattern around ip->scl assignment plus it_8026B390(gobj).
Used concrete HSD types where evidence supported them, such as HSD_JObj* for box/Samus grapple fields and HSD_JObjRemoveAll for box destruction cleanup.
decomp_lessons: When a motion-vars union field appears to need two incompatible types, do not force one type globally; add state-specific union members so each action state's layout can produce correct stores and loads.
Before changing a shared struct field type, test other callsites that use the same union memory; codegen regressions such as stfs versus stw can reveal that the field has state-specific meaning.
Fake matches should be labeled as @todo rather than neutral @note when the source suggests an unresolved inline or artificial reload pattern.
Repeated loads of gobj->user_data into different registers can indicate an inline function boundary rather than a natural local-variable pattern.

Self-assignment and PAD_STACK may be acceptable matching tactics when needed for register allocation or stack shape, but they should be treated as evidence of matching constraints rather than semantic source intent.
Item variable structs often need precise object-pointer fields, not just numeric placeholders; matching spawn/destroy callbacks can expose HSD_JObj ownership and cleanup responsibilities.
Reviewers may accept a fake match if it is explicitly documented and not misleading, especially when the suspected real inline has not been found elsewhere in the codebase.
naming_conventions: State-specific fighter motion-vars members should be named by state semantics when known, as in ftSamus_GrappleVars and the comment 'SpecialN charge shot state'.
New item data-attribute structs followed item-prefixed names such as itHarisen_DatAttrs and itFlipper_DatAttrs.
New item variable structs followed item-prefixed names such as itWstar_ItemVars.
Existing unknown function names remained address-based, such as it_802BABB8, it_802E8ADC, and grAnime_801C787C, rather than inventing unsupported descriptive names.
Callback names preserved project logic-table style, such as it_3F14_Logic20_Spawned, it_3F14_Logic20_Thrown, it_3F14_Logic24_Dropped, and it_3F14_Logic1_Destroyed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in several matched functions to preserve stack layout, including flipper spawned, harisen dropped, Kirby Dr. Mario hat loading, and Samus grapple duration setup.
Used a deliberate self-assignment pattern in ftKb_SpecialN_800F130C, `Fighter* fp = fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj);`, documented as required for register allocation.
Kept an explicit reload of `ip = gobj->user_data` in it_3F14_Logic24_Dropped to match a double-load pattern, while documenting it as a likely fake match caused by a missing inline.
Matched Samus grapple duration store by casting a data attribute value to float and storing it into the grapple-specific motion-var union member.
Preserved integer stores for Samus SpecialN frame counter by keeping the existing unk3.x4 field as s32.
Used bitmask accumulation in grAnime_801C787C to match flag behavior from arg2 bits before calling grAnime_801C752C.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned whether notes were AI-generated and said the comment should be @todo rather than @note because it suggested a fake match; also identified a likely inline function beginning around the second assignment.

review_feedback.author: malvarezcastillo
review_feedback.feedback: Acknowledged the PR was heavily AI-aided and said future PRs would mention this more clearly.

review_feedback.author: malvarezcastillo
review_feedback.feedback: Changed the harisen comment to @todo with more detail, describing the double user_data load at offsets 0x2c0 and 0x2c8 as an inline boundary and admitting the explicit reload was a fake match.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Said there was no need to drop the harisen fake match once it was documented.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Asked what code would break if the field type changed and requested @todo if no solution was found.

review_feedback.author: malvarezcastillo
review_feedback.feedback: Reported that changing unk3.x4 from s32 to float broke Samus SpecialN functions by producing float stores, then proposed a grapple-specific union member.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Confirmed the correct approach was to use a union type for each state and pointed to ftYoshi/types.h as an example.

searchable_terms: Match item callbacks and misc functions
follow_up_queries: Search for other item callbacks with `ip->scl = ip->xCC_item_attr->x60_scale; it_8026B390(gobj);` to confirm or name the suspected inline helper.
Audit remaining ftSamus_MotionVars members for action-state-specific names and types instead of generic unk structs.
Compare ftSamus motion-var union layout against ftYoshi/types.h per-state union pattern as reviewer suggested.
Review remaining item variable structs near xDD4 fields for placeholders that should be HSD_JObj* or state-specific structs.
Check whether other AI-aided matches include @note comments that should be @todo fake-match documentation.
